methods.description: This method involves selecting and applying appropriate normalization techniques for relative paleointensity (RPI) measurements in sediment cores, considering the variability in magnetic mineral assemblages and sediment matrix.

steps.description: Traditionally RPI has been estimated by normalizing NRM intensity with a single concentration-dependent rock magnetic parameter such as ARM, IRM or κ, generally after light AF demagnetization (20-30 mT) eliminating viscous or technically acquired soft magnetization components.
steps.searchable_summary: Normalize NRM intensity using ARM, IRM, or κ after AF demagnetization

steps.description: Tauxe et al. (1995) proposed a pseudo-Thellier approach, where NRM is plotted against ARM as both are sequentially demagnetized at the same AF field steps.
steps.searchable_summary: Apply pseudo-Thellier approach by plotting NRM against ARM

steps.description: The RPI value is determined as the best-fit slope to a quasi-linear curve section.
steps.searchable_summary: Determine RPI value from best-fit slope of NRM vs ARM plot

steps.description: Following the method of Chen et al. (2017), their 'detrital' 10-20 mT coercivity range would create a RPI curve with low amplitude dynamics.
steps.searchable_summary: Apply detrital coercivity range for RPI curve

steps.description: We opted for a broad and constant NRM 20-80mT /ARM 20-80mT slope range to normalize the three SO264 cores.
steps.searchable_summary: Normalize using broad NRM 20-80mT /ARM 20-80mT slope range

methods.method_name: SEM and TEM Analysis of Magnetic Extracts
methods.description: This method involves using Scanning Electron Microscopy (SEM) and Transmission Electron Microscopy (TEM) to analyze magnetic extracts from sediment samples, identifying and characterizing magnetic mineral particles.

steps.description: About 2 g sediments of each of the above listed SO264-19-2 samples was suspended in water and cycled through the magnetic extraction apparatus.
steps.searchable_summary: Extract magnetic particles from sediment samples using magnetic extraction apparatus

steps.description: The larger (>1 µm) lithogenic magnetic mineral particles were resolved by SEM and geochemically analyzed with EDX.
steps.searchable_summary: Analyze lithogenic magnetic particles using SEM and EDX

steps.description: Submicron (1-300 nm) sized magnetite particles were visualized and identified by TEM.
steps.searchable_summary: Visualize and identify submicron magnetite particles using TEM
